markdown:
| Personality Profile Questions |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 1/GentleTame, mild, meek,Gentle describes a natural disposition; a temper which is, by nature, not easily provoked. | 2/AttractivePleasing to the eye or mind especially through beauty or charm; “an attractive personality”. | 3/Easily ledBoldFearless and daring. | 4/Open-mindedReady to entertain new ideas. |
| Persuasive.Capable of convincing; having the power to induce action or belief;“a most persuasive speaker”. | God-fearing.Devoutly religious;“a god-fearing and law-abiding people” | LoyalSteadfast in allegiance or duty; faithful to law and true to the lawful government; unswerving in allegiance. | ObligingHappy to comply, showing a cheerful willingness to do favors for others; |
| OriginalAn original thinker or writer; an originator. | Stubborn.Tenaciously unwilling or marked by tenacious unwillingness to yield. | CharmingTo attract irresistibly; to delight exceedingly; to enchant; to fascinate. To enchant. | Will powerDetermination, fortitude, iron will,moral courage, strength of mind, strength of purpose, will of iron. |
| HumbleMarked by meekness or modesty; not arrogant or prideful. | SweetMildness and sweet reasonableness.A voice tremulous, but powerful.Pleasing to the eye. |  | CheerfulHaving or showing good spirits or joy; cheering; cheery; contented; happy; joyful. |
| 5/JovialGay; merry; joyous; jolly; hilarious; bright and cheerful. | 6/CompetitiveConsiderateCareful of the rights, claims and feelings of others. Thoughtful; reflective; careful; discreet; deliberate; serious. | 7/FussyMaking a fuss; disposed to make an unnecessary ado about trifles; overnice; fidgety,exacting,especially about details; | 8/BraveBold; courageous; daring; intrepid. |
| PreciseStrictly adhering or conforming to rule; in conduct or ceremony; formal; ceremonious. | JoyfulFull of joy; having or causing joy; very glad; a joyful heart. | ObedientSubject in will or act to authority; willing to obey; submissive to restraint, control, or command. | InspiringBright with promise, cheerful, cheery,dignified, encouraging, exhilarating, full of promise. |
| NervyBeing in a tense state\[syn: edgy, high-strung,jittery, jumpy,overstrung, restive,uptight\] | HarmoniousActing together to a common end; agreeing in action or feeling; living in peace and friendship. | UnconquerableNot capable of being conquered or vanquished or overcome;“a tribute to his courage...and his unconquerable will”.R.E.Danielson;“faced unconquerable difficulties” | SubmissiveInclined or willing to submit to orders or wishes of others or showing such inclination. |
| Even-temperedNot easily irritated; placid temperament\[syn: equable, good-tempered, placid\] |  | PlayfulFull of fun and high spirits. | TimidShowing fear and lack of confidence, lacking conviction or boldness or courage. |

* * *

| 9/SociableFriendly and pleasant.Inclined to or conducive to companionship with others. | 10/AdventurousWilling to undertake or seeking out new and daring enterprises. | 11/TalkativeFriendly and open and willing to talk;sometimes unwisely talking too much | 12/PolishedPolished manners;showing a high degree of refinement and the assurance that comes from wide social experience;“his polished manner”. |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| PatientExpectant with calmness,or without discontent;not hasty;not overeager;composed. | ReceptiveOpen to arguments,ideas,or change;“receptive to reason andthe logic of facts.” | ControlledCurbed or regulated;restrained or managedor kept within certainbounds;“controlledemotions” | DaringTo have adequate or sufficient courage forany purpose;to be boldor venturesome;not tobe afraid;to venture. |
| Self-reliantReliant upon one's self;trusting to one's ownpowers or judgment. | CordialHearty; sincere;warm;affectionate. | ConventionalConforming withaccepted standards;following acceptedcustoms and proprieties. | DiplomaticSkilled in dealing with sensitive matters orpeople |
| Soft-spokenSpeaking softly;havinga mild or gentle voice;hence,mild;affable. | ModerateMarked by avoidance ofextravagance orextremes;“moderatein his demands”;“restrained in hisresponse” | DecisiveCharacterizedbydecision and firmness;“an able an decisiveyoung woman”;shegave him a decisiveanswer | SatisfiedAccepting,assured,at ease,believing,certaincomfortable,composedconfident,content,convinced. |
| 13/AggressiveHaving or showingdetermination andenergetic pursuit ofyour ends;marked byaggressive ambition andenergy and initiative. | 14/CautiousCautious in attitude andcareful in actions;prudent;unwilling totake risks. | 15/WillingDisposed or inclinedtoward;“a willingparticipant”;“willinghelpers”. | 16/ConfidentHaving or marked byconfidence or assurance;“confident of fulfilment”“a confident speaker”. |
| Life-of-the-partyEasy markA defenceless victim,sitting duck,easy target,fall guy,greenhorn,pushover,trusting soul,victim. | DeterminedDecided,resolute.Strongly motivated to succeed | EagerHaving or showing keeninterest or intense desireor impatient expectancy;“eager to learn”;“eagerfor success”. | SympatheticExpressing or feelingor resulting from sympathyor compassion orfriendly fellow feelings. |
| FearfulLacking courage;ignobly timid and faint-hearted;timid by natureor revealing timidity. | ConvincingCapable of convincingor persuading;causingone to believe the truthof something; | AgreeableWilling;ready to agreeor consent. | TolerantShowing respect for therights or opinions orpractices of others. |
|  | Good-naturedNaturally mild in temper;not easilyprovoked. | High-spiritedFull of spirit or naturalfire;haughty;courageous;impetuous;not brooking restraint or opposition. | AssertiveInclined to bold andconfident assertion;aggressivelyself-assured;confidentlyaggressive. |

* * *

\| 17/Well-disciplinedAccustomed to regular and systematic action;good self-control to act systematically.
GenerousWilling to give and share, not petty in character and mind;“unusually generous in his judgment of people”
AnimatedFull of life or spirit;indicating animation;lively;vigorous.“An animated and expressive face;”animated conversation”.
PersistentInclined to persist;having staying qualities,tenacious of position or purpose.
21/TrustingInclined to believe or confide readily, full of trust.
ContentedSatisfied or showing satisfaction with things as they are.
PositiveFully assured; confident;certain;sometimes,overconfident;dogmatic;overbearing;-said of persons.
PeacefulNot disturbed by strifeor turmoil or war.Peacefully resistant in response to injustice \| 18/AdmirableHaving qualities to excite wonder,deserving the highest praise;most excellent;--used of persons“Admirable fortitude.”
KindShowing tenderness or goodness;disposed to do good and confer happiness;
ResignedSubmissive;yielding;not disposed to resist or murmur.having come to accept;“resigned to his fate”.
Force of characterStrength of mind;resolution;independence;individuality. \| 19/RespectfulAccommodating,agreeable,conscientiousconsiderate,cordial,courteous,dutiful,ethical,gentlemanly ladylike,obliging,well-behaved,well-mannered.
PioneeringTo go before,and prepare or open a way for;to act as pioneer.
OptimisticThe opinion that all events are ordered for the best.Expecting the best.
AccommodatingObliging;willing to do favours. \| 20/ArgumentativeGiven to argument,“argumentative to the point of being cantankerous”.
AdaptableCapable of adapting(of becoming or being made suitable) to a particular situation or use,“to succeed one must be adaptable”.
NonchalantIndifferent;careless;cool.

Light-heartedFree from grief or anxiety;gay;cheerful;merry.Carefree and happy. \|
\| \-\-\- \| \-\-\- \| \-\-\- \| \-\-\- \|
\| 22/Good mixerA person who has social intercourse with others of many sorts;a person viewed as to his casual sociability.
CulturedCharacterized by mental and moral training; disciplined; refined;well educated.
VigorousStrong and active physically or mentally.
LenientNot strict;characterized by tolerance and mercy,easy standards;“lenient rules”. \| 23/CompanionableFit for good fellowship;agreeable;sociable.Friendly,hospital,sociable,social-minded.
AccurateConforming exactly or almost exactly to fact or to a standard or performing with total accuracy.
OutspokenGiven to expressing yourself freely or insistently;characterized by directness in manner or speech;without subtlety or evasion;“blunt talker,straight shooter”.
RestrainedCool and formal in manner,not showy or obtrusive;prudent;“guarded optimism” \| 24/RestlessUnable to relax or be still;not satisfied to be at rest or in peace;averse to repose or quiet;eager for change.
NeighbourlyAgreeable,amitable,civil,considerate,cordial,courteous,friendly,gracious,harmonious,helpful,kind,thoughtful,well disposed.
PopularRegarded with great favour,approval,or affection.
DevoutDevoted to religion or to religious feelings and duties;“a god-fearing and law-abiding people.” \| \|
